output.question: Fetch https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Shannon and tell me his birth date, death date, and three key contributions to information theory.

sources.title: A Mathematical Theory of Communication - Wikipedia

output.findings: Claude Elwood Shannon was born on April 30, 1916, in Petoskey, Michigan, and died on February 24, 2001, in Cambridge, Massachusetts. His most influential contributions to information theory are: (1) The 1948 paper "A Mathematical Theory of Communication," which introduced the concept of information entropy and provided a quantitative framework for communication systems. (2) The definition of the bit (binary digit) as the fundamental unit of information, establishing the binary basis for modern digital communication and computing. (3) The formulation of the channel capacity theorem (Shannon limit), which determines the maximum reliable data transmission rate over a noisy channel and underpins modern coding theory and data compression.

output.final_answer: Claude Shannon was born on April 30, 1916, and died on February 24, 2001. His three key contributions to information theory are: (1) the 1948 paper "A Mathematical Theory of Communication," which introduced information entropy and a quantitative framework for communication; (2) defining the bit as the fundamental unit of information, establishing the binary basis for digital communication and computing; and (3) formulating the channel capacity theorem (Shannon limit), which determines the maximum reliable data transmission rate over a noisy channel and underpins modern coding theory and data compression.
